events.label: reasoning
events.text: We need answer JSON only. Need decide pass/fail/needs_more_info. Scenario: Pipe bedding & backfill, observed field condition: lifts too thick for compactor. Work rough-complete. Reference: project specs, IPC 306. Need assess compliance. Lifts too thick for compactor means backfill placed in lifts exceeding maximum thickness allowed for the compaction equipment/method, resulting in inadequate compaction, potential settlement, pipe support issues. This is non-compliant. Decision fail. Risk high? Industrial site pipe bedding/backfill inadequate compaction can cause settlement, pipe stress, failure. Could be high. Component conditions: installed-defective, non-compliant. Percent complete rough-complete maybe 70-80. Value not asked 0. Sound etc default 0/empty. Findings: lifts too thick for compactor; likely inadequate compaction. Actions: remove/rework lifts to specified thickness, recompact per spec/IPC 306, verify compaction testing. Rationale: bedding/backfill must be placed in lifts compatible with compactor and spec; too thick prevents required density and support. References: project specs, IPC 306. Need ensure JSON valid. No prose outside.

    "Stop backfill placement in the affected area.",
    "Remove or scarify over-thick lifts to the maximum lift thickness specified/compatible with the compactor.",

    "Verify compaction with appropriate density tests before proceeding."
  ],
  "rationale": "Pipe bedding and backfill must be placed in lifts that allow the selected compaction equipment to achieve the specified density and provide uniform support. Lifts that are too thick for the compactor result in inadequate compaction, non-compliant support, and risk of settlement or pipe damage.",
